Package neqsim.process.equipment.distillation

Class ColumnSolverFactory

java.lang.Object
neqsim.process.equipment.distillation.ColumnSolverFactory
final class ColumnSolverFactory extends Object
Factory for the built-in distillation column solver strategies.

This class keeps enum dispatch outside DistillationColumn. The first implementation is a thin adapter layer around the existing numerical methods so that the public behavior remains unchanged while future rigorous solvers can be added as separate strategies.

Version:
1.0
Author:
esol

      MESH residual-monitored strategy.

    • AUTO

      private static final ColumnSolver AUTO
      Automatic strategy selector.

    • verifyAcceleratedResults

      private static volatile boolean verifyAcceleratedResults
      Whether accelerated solvers (Wegstein, Sum-Rates, Naphtali-Sandholm, MESH residual) should run a damped-substitution validation solve after a successful solve and roll back to the damped result if product splits differ materially. Off by default because the validation solve is typically as expensive as the accelerator itself, halving the wallclock speedup. The accelerators already fall back internally when DistillationColumn.solved() is not satisfied, so this extra layer is only useful for regression auditing.

    • autoCandidateProbeMode

      private static final ThreadLocal<Boolean> autoCandidateProbeMode
      Thread-local marker used while AUTO probes candidate solvers. Candidate probes should report their own state without spending another full damped-substitution run inside each rejected candidate; AUTO applies the robust fallback once after ranking the probes.

  • Constructor Details

    • ColumnSolverFactory

      private ColumnSolverFactory()
      Utility class constructor.

  • Method Details

    • setVerifyAcceleratedResults

      static void setVerifyAcceleratedResults(boolean enabled)
      Enable or disable the damped-substitution verification step run after successful accelerated solves. Disabled by default.
      Parameters:
      enabled - true to verify every accelerated result against a damped fallback

    • isVerifyAcceleratedResults

      static boolean isVerifyAcceleratedResults()
      Check whether accelerated result verification is currently enabled.
      Returns:
      true if every accelerated solve is verified against a damped solve

    • create

      static ColumnSolver create(DistillationColumn.SolverType solverType)
      Create the strategy for a solver type.
      Parameters:
      solverType - requested solver type
      Returns:
      solver strategy for the requested type

    • createDampedFallbackCandidate

      private static DistillationColumn createDampedFallbackCandidate(DistillationColumn column)
      Create a clean damped-substitution fallback candidate before an accelerator changes tray state.
      Parameters:
      column - column to copy
      Returns:
      copied column, or null if the copy fails

    • createAutoCandidate

      private static DistillationColumn createAutoCandidate(DistillationColumn column)
      Create a candidate copy for the automatic selector.
      Parameters:
      column - source column
      Returns:
      copied column, or null if copying is unavailable

    • prepareAutoCandidate

      private static void prepareAutoCandidate(DistillationColumn candidate, DistillationColumn.SolverType candidateSolver)
      Prepare an automatic-solver candidate with robust defaults.
      Parameters:
      candidate - candidate column copy to prepare
      candidateSolver - solver to apply to the candidate

    • selectCandidateSolvers

      private static DistillationColumn.SolverType[] selectCandidateSolvers(DistillationColumn column)
      Select candidate solvers for automatic mode.
      Parameters:
      column - column being solved
      Returns:
      ordered candidate solver types, excluding DistillationColumn.SolverType.AUTO

    • hasAdjustableProductSpecification

      private static boolean hasAdjustableProductSpecification(DistillationColumn column)
      Check whether the column has a product specification that benefits from continuation.
      Parameters:
      column - column to inspect
      Returns:
      true when a purity, recovery, or product-flow specification is active

    • shouldUseAutomaticShortcutSeed

      private static boolean shouldUseAutomaticShortcutSeed(DistillationColumn column)
      Check whether AUTO should pay for a shortcut-column rebuild before probing candidates.

      Fenske-Underwood-Gilliland seeding is valuable for product-specification continuation and larger full fractionators, but small fixed-specification regression columns are usually solved faster from a temperature-profile seed.

      Parameters:
      column - column being solved
      Returns:
      true when shortcut initialization is expected to help

    • isAdjustableProductSpecification

      private static boolean isAdjustableProductSpecification(ColumnSpecification specification)
      Check whether one specification is adjusted through the outer temperature loop.
      Parameters:
      specification - specification to inspect
      Returns:
      true for purity, recovery, or product-flow specifications

    • runAutoWarmBase

      private static ColumnSolveResult runAutoWarmBase(DistillationColumn candidateSource, UUID id, StringBuilder summary)
      Run the relaxed base stage used by automatic solver mode.
      Parameters:
      candidateSource - candidate source column to warm start
      id - calculation identifier
      summary - automatic solver summary builder
      Returns:
      solve result from the relaxed base stage

    • runAutoProbeCandidate

      private static ColumnSolveResult runAutoProbeCandidate(DistillationColumn candidate, DistillationColumn.SolverType candidateSolver, UUID id)
      Run one AUTO candidate probe without allowing the candidate wrapper to perform its own damped fallback solve.
      Parameters:
      candidate - candidate column copy
      candidateSolver - solver to probe
      id - calculation identifier
      Returns:
      result reported by the probed solver before AUTO-level fallback

    • isAutoCandidateProbeMode

      private static boolean isAutoCandidateProbeMode()
      Check whether the current strategy call is an AUTO probe.
      Returns:
      true when damped fallback work should be deferred to AUTO

    • shouldPrepareAcceleratedFallback

      private static boolean shouldPrepareAcceleratedFallback()
      Check whether an accelerator should prepare a damped validation/fallback copy.
      Returns:
      true when accelerated-result verification is enabled outside AUTO probing

    • autoProbeNote

      private static String autoProbeNote(DistillationColumn.SolverType candidateSolver, DistillationColumn candidate, ColumnSolveResult result)
      Build an explanatory note for an AUTO candidate probe.
      Parameters:
      candidate - candidate column after the probe
      result - probe result
      Returns:
      note for the AUTO summary, or null when no note is needed

    • isAcceptableAutoCandidate

      private static boolean isAcceptableAutoCandidate(DistillationColumn candidate, ColumnSolveResult result)
      Check whether an AUTO candidate can be accepted without running the live fallback.
      Parameters:
      candidate - candidate column after the probe
      result - probe result
      Returns:
      true when the candidate is solved and did not rely on fallback products

    • shouldAcceptWarmBaseResult

      private static boolean shouldAcceptWarmBaseResult(DistillationColumn column, DistillationColumn candidate, ColumnSolveResult result)
      Check whether AUTO can stop after the relaxed base solve.

      Fixed-specification, small condenser/reboiler columns usually do not benefit from extra simultaneous-correction probes once the warmed damped solve already satisfies the active tolerances. Accepting the base result avoids copying and flashing the same column for every candidate while preserving the full homotopy/probe ladder for product specifications and larger columns.

      Parameters:
      column - live AUTO column
      candidate - warmed candidate source
      result - warmed base result
      Returns:
      true when the warmed base solve is a sufficient AUTO result

    • appendAutoFeasibilitySummary

      private static void appendAutoFeasibilitySummary(StringBuilder summary, ValidationResult feasibility)
      Append feasibility pre-screen status to the automatic solver trace.
      Parameters:
      summary - summary builder receiving a one-line status
      feasibility - feasibility validation result

    • runAutoFallbackOnLiveColumn

      private static ColumnSolveResult runAutoFallbackOnLiveColumn(DistillationColumn column, UUID id, StringBuilder summary)
      Run the robust fallback directly on the live column when trial copies are unavailable.
      Parameters:
      column - live column
      id - calculation identifier
      summary - automatic solver summary builder
      Returns:
      solve result from the fallback solver

    • appendAutoCandidateSummary

      private static void appendAutoCandidateSummary(StringBuilder summary, DistillationColumn.SolverType candidateSolver, ColumnSolveResult result, String note)
      Append one candidate attempt to the automatic solver trace.
      Parameters:
      summary - summary builder to append to
      candidateSolver - solver requested for the candidate
      result - candidate result, or null when no result is available
      note - optional explanatory note

    • scoreResult

      private static double scoreResult(ColumnSolveResult result, DistillationColumn candidate)
      Score a candidate result for best-effort automatic fallback.
      Parameters:
      result - candidate result
      candidate - candidate column, or null if only the result is available
      Returns:
      finite scalar score where lower is better

    • residualScore

      private static double residualScore(double residual)
      Convert a residual to a finite scoring contribution.
      Parameters:
      residual - candidate residual value
      Returns:
      finite non-negative residual score

    • applyDampedFallback

      private static void applyDampedFallback(DistillationColumn column, DistillationColumn fallbackCandidate, UUID id, String reason, RuntimeException exception)
      Apply damped substitution from a clean candidate when an accelerator is rejected.
      Parameters:
      column - live column to update
      fallbackCandidate - clean fallback candidate, or null if unavailable
      id - calculation identifier
      reason - rejection reason
      exception - optional accelerator exception

    • validateAcceleratedProductSplit

      private static void validateAcceleratedProductSplit(DistillationColumn column, DistillationColumn fallbackCandidate, UUID id, String solverName)
      Validate an accelerator result against a damped-substitution candidate.
      Parameters:
      column - accelerated result
      fallbackCandidate - clean damped candidate, or null if unavailable
      id - calculation identifier
      solverName - name of the accelerator being validated

    • validateNaphtaliWarmStartProductSplit

      private static boolean validateNaphtaliWarmStartProductSplit(DistillationColumn column, DistillationColumn warmStartCandidate, UUID id)
      Keep the residual-monitored warm start when an initializer-only Naphtali-Sandholm candidate produces a materially different product split.
      Parameters:
      column - live column containing the Naphtali-Sandholm candidate result
      warmStartCandidate - clean candidate used to compute the guarded warm-start state
      id - calculation identifier
      Returns:
      true when the warm-start candidate was accepted on the live column

    • productSplitDiffers

      private static boolean productSplitDiffers(DistillationColumn accelerated, DistillationColumn reference)
      Check whether two solved columns expose materially different product splits.
      Parameters:
      accelerated - accelerated result
      reference - reference damped-substitution result
      Returns:
      true when gas or liquid product flows differ beyond tolerance

    • differsByMoreThanProductTolerance

      private static boolean differsByMoreThanProductTolerance(double actual, double reference)
      Compare two product flow values with a relative tolerance.
      Parameters:
      actual - accelerated value
      reference - reference value
      Returns:
      true when the values differ materially
